sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager or his designee to amend Contract No.
32400177 with Dr. Lisa B. Hernandez to increase by $60,000 for a total not to exceed
amount (NTE) of $180,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Costs for Health Officer coverage will be paid for by General Funds (Fund 011) salary
savings in the Fiscal Year 2025 Adopted Budget. The grant (Fund 302) that supported
this contract last year was reduced for all jurisdictions statewide due to budget cuts.
Payment will be $50 per hour/$1,200 per day. Total costs are not to exceed $180,000.
sections.current_situation:
The City of Berkeley receives funding from the California Department of Public Health
through the Future of Public Health grant. The funding requires each local health
jurisdiction to have Health Officer on-call coverage at all times. The City of Berkeley
currently relies on backup coverage while the City is recruiting a permanent Health
Officer.
sections.background:
Most public health jurisdictions have on-call Health Officer coverage to address urgent
communicable diseases and other public health issues. Additionally, this coverage is
required by the Future of Public Health grant. Dr. Hernandez served as Health Officer
for the City of Berkeley for five years, until July 2023. The City Council is asked to
approve continued Health Officer coverage, as Council also approves the appointment
of a permanent Health Officer.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects, climate impacts, or sustainability
opportunities associated with the subject of this report.

Page 2 of 3
Contract Amendment – Contract No. 34200177 for CONSENT CALENDAR
Health Officer Coverage-Dr. Lisa Hernandez March 11, 2025
sections.rationale:
Health Officer coverage is required by the Future of Public Health grant and may be
needed if urgent public health issues arise.
sections.alternatives:
The City could continue to have periodic gaps in Health Officer coverage when the
Health Officer is unavailable.
CONTACT PERSON
Alexandra Deitch, Sr. Health Services Program Specialist, HHCS, 510-981-5379
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32400177 AMENDMENT: DR. LISA B. HERNANDEZ FOR HEALTH
OFFICER SERVICES DURING PERIODS WHEN THE CITY HEALTH OFFICER IS
OUT OF OFFICE
WHEREAS, most public health jurisdictions have on-call Health Officer coverage to
address urgent communicable diseases and other public health issues; and
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley receives funding from the California Department of
Public Health through the Future of Public Health grant, which requires each local health
jurisdiction to have Health Officer coverage at all times; and
WHEREAS, Dr. Lisa B. Hernandez served as the Health Officer for the City of Berkeley
for five years, and is willing to provide coverage for the City of Berkeley again; and
WHEREAS, on April 1, 2024, the City of Berkeley entered into contract with Dr.
Hernandez (Contract 32400177) to provide Health Officer coverage when the Health
Officer is out of the office to ensure 24/7 Health Officer coverage; and
WHEREAS, an additional $60,000 is needed to increase the existing contract for Health
Officer coverage for the City of Berkeley when the Health Officer is out of the office and
unavailable.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager or his designee is authorized to amend Contract No. 32400177 and any
amendments with Dr. Lisa Hernandez from April 1, 2024, to June 30, 2026, in an amount
not to exceed $180,000 utilizing General Funds (Fund 011) and Future of Public Health
Grant Funds (Fund 302).
